verdict_reasoning: SRC (c-Src) is canonically a non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and a polybasic cluster — not accessible from the extracellular face under baseline conditions. However, a 2026 Science study demonstrated that in cancer cells, Src undergoes topological inversion onto the cell surface via autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE), making it accessible from the extracellular face as 'eSrc'. Anti-Src antibody-based therapies were shown to mediate tumor cell killing in this context. This documented cancer-state surface presentation qualifies as contextual via cell_state_induced/lysosomal_exocytosis.
